aPlusContent.title: From the Publisher
aPlusContent.rawText:
From the Publisher
Time-Tested Tools 
Our learning materials have helped educate three generations of kids, creating life-long learners, and the legacy continues. Digital content, flash cards, workbooks, readers, toys and games—all are crafted through a child’s eyes, while integrating parent and teacher standards. 
Writing by Hand Improves Memory 
Writing in workbook pages, as opposed to typing on a computer, can help kids focus and learn. Writing by hand fires up specific areas of a child's brain, improving the ability to both remember what he or she learns and to think of new ideas. 
Longer Retention 
Kids and parents who use our flash cards for practice testing are using one of the most effective learning techniques available. A hundred years of research shows practice testing is highly likely to boost retention. 
Reading Has Lifelong Benefits 
A love of reading has been proven to have far-reaching benefits for children, emotionally and academically. And children who enjoy reading become confident readers and read more often. 
Kids Love Technology 
School Zone’s digital content supports children’s learning goals by enhancing problem-solving and critical thinking, as well as reinforcing skills through multisensory activities. It also improves language skills, increases motivation to stay on task, and stimulates curiosity and imagination. 
Not Just for Fun 
When kids play games together, they learn a healthy sense of competition and experience the joy of winning and empathy for losing. Along the way, they learn collaboration, cooperation, and problem-solving. Creative play stimulates imagination and nurtures emotional well-being.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This was a support item I bought when I was homeschooling our daughter in 3rd grade.  Our primary math curriculum was Singapore math, which I highly recommend and can't say enough good things about (and yes, they do have a common core version of that), but she was struggling more with division and needed more practice then the workbooks we had for that provided, and I didn't have a bunch of time to scour the internet for additional worksheets.I found this to be perfect for that (though she, admittedly, was less then thrilled about the extra practice).  It had a variety of different worksheets, with division from memory based on math facts, to the beginnings of long division, to division with a remainder.  It worked well for the support we needed, and it was worth buying it to save a bit of my precious time.

aPlusContent.title: From the Publisher
aPlusContent.rawText:
From the Publisher
Time-Tested Tools 
Our learning materials have helped educate three generations of kids, creating life-long learners, and the legacy continues. Digital content, flash cards, workbooks, readers, toys and games—all are crafted through a child’s eyes, while integrating parent and teacher standards. 
Writing by Hand Improves Memory 
Writing in workbook pages, as opposed to typing on a computer, can help kids focus and learn. Writing by hand fires up specific areas of a child's brain, improving the ability to both remember what he or she learns and to think of new ideas. 
Longer Retention 
Kids and parents who use our flash cards for practice testing are using one of the most effective learning techniques available. A hundred years of research shows practice testing is highly likely to boost retention. 
Reading Has Lifelong Benefits 
A love of reading has been proven to have far-reaching benefits for children, emotionally and academically. And children who enjoy reading become confident readers and read more often. 
Kids Love Technology 
School Zone’s digital content supports children’s learning goals by enhancing problem-solving and critical thinking, as well as reinforcing skills through multisensory activities. It also improves language skills, increases motivation to stay on task, and stimulates curiosity and imagination. 
Not Just for Fun 
When kids play games together, they learn a healthy sense of competition and experience the joy of winning and empathy for losing. Along the way, they learn collaboration, cooperation, and problem-solving. Creative play stimulates imagination and nurtures emotional well-being.

aPlusContent.rawText:
From the Publisher
Provide a spectrum of support for 3rd graders as they learn multiplication, division, fractions, and more math skills with the Spectrum 3rd Grade Math Workbook! 
Correlated to State Standards, the Spectrum Grade 3 Math Workbook features 192 full-color pages with a variety of engaging 3rd grade math activities, reviews, and additional features, including: 
Parent support at the beginning of each chapter covering important definitions, tips, checklists, and more
Lesson/chapter reviews and tests 
Enrichment and critical thinking questions 
Vibrant illustrations for added visual support
Engaging online practice on IXL
Answer key
Supporting Growth With Checklists 
Skills checklists help you to keep track of the skills being learned along the way. 
Enhancing Vocabulary Skills 
Definitions are included to better understand vocabulary that correlates with the skills and concepts being taught throughout each chapter. 
Additional Learning Support 
Each chapter features tools and tips for additional ways to reinforce skills and support your learner. 
Reviewing Skills Along the Way 
Lesson and chapter reviews provide step-by-step instruction and examples followed by independent practice. 
Encourage Critical Thinking 
Enrichment and critical thinking problems encourage students to dig deeper and apply the skills they learned in different ways. 
Tracking Progress 
Checkpoints and tests allow you to track student progress along the way. 
Engaging Online Practice 
Skill ID’s bring learning to life with fun, interactive activities on IXL.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: As a mother of eight, finding the right educational resources for my children is incredibly important—and Spectrum workbooks have been an absolute game-changer in our home! With kids at different grade levels and learning styles, I need materials that are thorough, easy to follow, and engaging. Spectrum workbooks check all those boxes and more.What I love most is how well-organized and straightforward the lessons are. My children can work through them independently, but they’re also structured enough that I can easily step in and help when needed. The explanations are clear, the exercises reinforce key concepts beautifully, and there’s plenty of practice to ensure mastery. Whether it's math, reading, language arts, or writing, these books provide a solid foundation that truly helps my kids excel.Another huge plus is that they’re not overwhelming. The layout is clean, the instructions are simple, and my children actually enjoy working through them (which, as any parent knows, is a big deal!). I also appreciate the answer keys, which make it easy for me to check their progress without spending hours grading.Spectrum workbooks have become a staple in our homeschooling and supplemental learning routine. They’ve helped my children build confidence in their skills, and I’ve seen firsthand how effective they are. If you're a parent looking for high-quality, affordable workbooks that actually make a difference, I can't recommend Spectrum enough!
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 30, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Excellent Prep for 3rd Grade – We Love These Books!
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I originally purchased the reading book to help my child stay on track over the summer, and we loved it so much that I went back and bought the math book too! Both are under 150 pages, which makes them feel manageable—and they’ve quickly become part of our daily routine.We do one page a day, and it’s the perfect way to introduce new concepts without overwhelming my child. The reading book includes short, engaging passages with questions that build comprehension and vocabulary. The math book is just as impressive—it walks through key concepts for Grade 3 in a simple and encouraging way.What I really appreciate is how these books preview what’s coming up next year, so my child will feel more confident and ready when school starts again. They’re ideal for summer learning or even light review during the school year.Highly recommend for parents looking to keep learning fun and consistent!

The workbook is also aligned with common core standards, which gives me peace of mind that it’s relevant to what’s being taught in school.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Great Summer Math Practice for 3rd Graders!
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I picked up this Grade 3 Math book for my child to use over the summer, and it’s been a solid choice so far. I wanted something that would help keep their skills sharp before heading into the next school year, and this workbook covers all the right topics: multiplication, division, word problems, adding and subtracting 4-digit numbers, fractions, and even graphs.One thing I really like is how it’s laid out. My child can do a couple of pages a day and still enjoy their summer without it turning into a full-on homework session. The explanations are clear, and there are a few practice problems for each concept, so kids actually get a chance to reinforce what they’re learning.The word problems are a great bonus too!

productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewTitle: Using these for years - fabulous product
productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewDescription: Ordered in hard copy to Melbourne, Australia, during extended lockdowns resulting from the global pandemic. Ordinarily, I would purchase the eBook of the same, however couldn't get access to a printer during COVID lockdowns. Each practice page features purposeful illustrations to help learners visualize and solve problems in an easy-to-understand way.
Perfect for Homeschool and Classroom Learning: Parents love that this teacher-approved third grade workbook makes at-home learning easy, while teachers trust it for classroom, small-group use, summer learning, and intervention skill-building to address learning gaps.
Track and Support Progress With Ease: Helpful overview pages explain what children are learning and offer tips to support them throughout their learning journey. This math workbook also includes diagnostic tools and an answer key to easily track and assess progress and learning needs along the way.
About Our Best-Selling Workbook Series: The 180 Days™ of Practice series by Shell Education provides standards-based, daily activities that engage students in learning language, reading, writing, spelling, science, social studies, social-emotional learning, mathematics, and more! aPlusContent.title: From the Publisher
aPlusContent.rawText:
From the Publisher
180 Days: Third Grade -- Engaging Daily Practice Across Every Core Subject 
Quick, Easy-to-Use Lessons for Teachers and Parents 
Third grade marks a major turning point in a child’s education. This is the year students shift from learning to read to reading to learn, using their reading skills to understand new concepts in science, social studies, math, and beyond. Strong daily practice helps ensure they have the comprehension, vocabulary, and critical-thinking skills needed for this important transition. 
Shell Education's 180 Days for Third Grade workbooks provide ready-to-go activities that fit seamlessly into daily routines for homework, intervention, or enrichment. 
Third Grade: The Turning Point for Young Readers 
Expand Language Skills to Support Third Grade Success 
Third graders build essential word knowledge and language skills through daily, targeted practice that reinforces new concepts over time. As students expand their vocabulary, they develop stronger comprehension, clearer communication, and the confidence to tackle more complex texts across all subjects. 
Help Students Map Out Meaning in Every Lesson 
When third graders build solid reading comprehension skills, they’re better equipped to understand complex ideas in geography, science, and social studies. Strong vocabulary and decoding skills help them make sense of maps and informational texts, analyze details, and connect new concepts to what they already know. 
Stronger Reading Skills Lead to Deeper Learning in Every Subject 
By third grade, students must use reading to understand lessons, solve problems, and explore new topics. Strengthening comprehension now leads to better performance in every subject. Daily practice ensures students stay on track for long-term academic success.

Practice drawing shapes and dividing them into halves, thirds, and fourths  Clear examples, instructions, and goals help kids master and retain new math concepts Whimsical, colorful illustrations help kids focus and stay motivated Perforated pages are easy to tear out for individual worksheets IMAGINATION - Master the basics! By mastering essential third grade math skills, your child will feel more confident in the classroom. This 64-page Math Basics 3 workbook follows the Principles and Standards for School Mathematics, published by the National Council for Teachers of Mathematics (NCTM), and features interesting activities and colorful illustrations that introduce the basics covered in third grade math. The clear examples and instructions make it easy for kids to set their own and goals and help kids master and retain new math concepts in this kids math workbook. This 3rd grade workbook will also help your little learner with factors and prime numbers, math word problems, multiplication and division, fractions, and so much more! Your child can either work in the workbook itself or the perforated pages make it easy to tear out for individual worksheets. WORKBOOKS FOR KIDS - Using kids workbooks is a great way to improve learning. Writing by hand, as opposed to typing on a computer, fires up specific areas of a child's brain, improving their ability to not only remember what he or she learns but to think of new ideas. Completing physical pages also give a sense of accomplishment that’s hard to get from a digital product. This is a workbook kids are sure to enjoy! When kids finish the workbook, you can fill out the included “Great Job! You’re #1” completion certificate to reward a job well done. PREPARATION - Let us help you prepare your young children for the next grade level with our workbooks, flashcards, card games, early reading books, and online learning program – Anywhere Teacher. Keep kids "classroom ready" with these great supplemental learning tools and more!AWARD WINNING - School Zone content has won The Parents’ Choice Foundation Award, Tillywig Toy Awards, Brainchild Award, Family Choice Award, Mom's Choice Awards Honoring Excellence, Gold Star Toy Scholastic Parent and Child Award and many more.OUR STORY - More than 40 years ago, long-time educators James Hoffman, Ed.D., and his wife Joan Hoffman, M.A., began a retail supply outlet for teachers, then soon identified a need for at-home learning materials to help parents support and supplement their children’s classroom instruction. School Zone Publishing fast emerged as the market leader in workbooks and flashcards for preschoolers through sixth graders.School Zone remains family-owned, focused on excellence, responsive to customers, alert to emerging technology, and committed to the community. We continually update our products with new features and content, reflective of changing standards, evolving teaching methods, and overall best practices.

aPlusContent.title: From the Publisher
aPlusContent.rawText:
From the Publisher
Time-Tested Tools 
Our learning materials have helped educate three generations of kids, creating life-long learners, and the legacy continues. Digital content, flash cards, workbooks, readers, toys and games—all are crafted through a child’s eyes, while integrating parent and teacher standards. 
Writing by Hand Improves Memory 
Writing in workbook pages, as opposed to typing on a computer, can help kids focus and learn. Writing by hand fires up specific areas of a child's brain, improving the ability to both remember what he or she learns and to think of new ideas. 
Longer Retention 
Kids and parents who use our flash cards for practice testing are using one of the most effective learning techniques available. A hundred years of research shows practice testing is highly likely to boost retention. 
Reading Has Lifelong Benefits 
A love of reading has been proven to have far-reaching benefits for children, emotionally and academically. And children who enjoy reading become confident readers and read more often. 
Kids Love Technology 
School Zone’s digital content supports children’s learning goals by enhancing problem-solving and critical thinking, as well as reinforcing skills through multisensory activities. It also improves language skills, increases motivation to stay on task, and stimulates curiosity and imagination. 
Not Just for Fun 
When kids play games together, they learn a healthy sense of competition and experience the joy of winning and empathy for losing. Along the way, they learn collaboration, cooperation, and problem-solving. Creative play stimulates imagination and nurtures emotional well-being.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: The School Zone Math Basics 3 Workbook is an excellent resource for third-grade students (ages 8 to 9). With 64 pages of engaging exercises, it covers essential math concepts like multiplication, division, word problems, place value, and fractions. The layout is clear and age-appropriate, providing a structured approach to reinforce fundamental skills. It's a valuable tool for parents and educators seeking to support a child's mathematical development in a fun and educational way.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on December 25, 2023

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I purchased this workbook to give my son a preview of 3rd grade over summer vacation.  I really love this booklet and for the price, it cannot be beat!It consists of 56 pages of math activities with an Answer Key in the back.  The last page is a certificate for the child after completion.  The back of the booklet provides additional activities to reinforce the math concepts learned.It is divided into sections (4 digit addition & subtraction, multiplication, division, etc.).  At the beginning of each section, a brief summary is provided to show demonstrate the math concept.  I found this to be very helpful as a parent since the way math is now taught is different from how I learned.  It can be confusing for a child to learn a method at home when different terminology is used by school ("regrouping" versus "take away" when subtracting).  It really helps for the parent and teacher to be on the same page when learning something new.I highly recommend this booklet for review or preparation for 3rd Grade math!

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Great, cheap exercise book for grade subject math.  Be sure to get Deluxe Edition.
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: What is great about these books is they seem to cover the majority of the grade level math with a few pages of exercise problems at a very cheap price - approx. $3.  The Deluxe Edition contains 2x more content than the less expensive NCTM books ($2), so be cautious when you order.  You will notice the "Deluxe Edition" label is printed on the front cover upper RH corner of the book.  This Grade 3 Math Basics book happens to be a Deluxe Edition version.If you want more practice exercises for your children, then Kumon offers great workbooks based on grade and math subject.  Here are some 3rd grade examples:Word Problems (Kumon Math Workbooks Grade 3),Grade 3 Division (Kumon Math Workbooks),Grade 3 Multiplication (Kumon Math Workbooks),Geometry & Measurement, Grade 3,Grade 3 Addition & Subtraction (Kumon Math Workbooks),If you are home schooling and your children need lots of practice exercises, then go with Kumon.  Kumon cannot be beat for exercise practice!  Some other really good books that Amazon sells which target specific math topics and give you lots of practice exercises are produced by Chris McMullen.  Do a search with his name in Amazon to find his books.If you just want a little practice material for your kids to do during the school year, or summertime, then this book would fit the bill at a lower cost than Kumon.  You can also find free practice worksheets on the web, search for Math Drills.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 20, 2013
